_meta.description: Curated directory of Cincinnati civic organizations, surfaced contextually alongside relevant data. Maintained by hand — no API will replace local knowledge of who works on what in this city.

service_types.direct_service: Direct Service — provides help directly to individuals
service_types.organizing: Organizing & Advocacy — builds community power for systemic change

_meta.note: Verified April 2026. Some contact details may change — always confirm on the org's website before referring residents.

organizations.name: Legal Aid Society of Greater Cincinnati
organizations.mission: If you've received an eviction notice or are in a landlord dispute, call them first. They provide free legal representation to low-income residents and can appear in court with you.

organizations.name: Housing Opportunities Made Equal (HOME Cincinnati)
organizations.mission: If a landlord denied you housing, raised your rent suddenly, or treated you differently because of your race, family status, or disability — HOME can file a fair housing complaint on your behalf, for free.

organizations.name: Over-the-Rhine Community Housing
organizations.mission: If you want to stay in OTR or nearby neighborhoods as rents rise, OTRCH maintains permanently affordable rental units. Contact them about availability or to get on a waiting list.

organizations.name: Price Hill Will
organizations.mission: If you live in Price Hill and need housing rehab help, local resource connections, or want to get involved in shaping your neighborhood — Price Hill Will runs community programs and organizing.

organizations.name: Communities Act Now (CAN)
organizations.mission: If your neighborhood is facing buyouts, displacement, predatory lending, or biased policing, CAN organizes collective neighborhood responses. Join a member network to add your voice to the fight.

organizations.name: Cincinnati-Hamilton County Community Action Agency
organizations.mission: If you can't pay rent or utilities this month, call the CAA for emergency assistance. They serve households in crisis and can sometimes help prevent eviction.

organizations.name: Greater Cincinnati Water Works — Lead Program
organizations.mission: If your service line is lead or unknown, call or email to get it replaced at no cost to you. The city covers 100% of replacement costs. They also provide free water testing kits.

organizations.name: Cincinnati Health Department — Lead Poisoning Prevention
organizations.mission: If you have a child under 6, call for a free blood lead test and free water filters. They can also coordinate lead abatement for your home at no cost.

organizations.name: Groundwork Ohio River Valley
organizations.mission: If you're concerned about air quality or environmental conditions in your neighborhood, Groundwork monitors local air and runs community programs. Contact them to get involved or report a concern.

organizations.name: Green Umbrella
organizations.mission: If you want to connect with environmental advocacy efforts across the region, Green Umbrella convenes the Cincinnati-area sustainability network and can connect you with groups working on your specific concern.

organizations.name: Cincinnati NAACP
organizations.mission: If you've experienced police misconduct or a civil rights violation, the NAACP can support documentation, legal referrals, and public advocacy. They also run voter registration and community rights education.

organizations.name: Freestore Foodbank
organizations.mission: If you need food, use their website to find the nearest pantry or mobile market — they serve 110,000+ people annually across the region with no income verification required at most locations.

organizations.name: Society of St. Vincent de Paul Cincinnati
organizations.mission: If you need emergency food, rent help, or utility assistance, find your nearest SVdP location — 13 parish-based sites across Cincinnati provide direct help, often same day.

organizations.name: Sustainable Cincy
organizations.mission: If you want Cincinnati to invest in better transit, safer streets, or walkable neighborhoods, Sustainable Cincy coordinates regional advocacy campaigns. Sign up for action alerts to weigh in on key decisions.

organizations.name: ChangingGears
organizations.mission: If you need a car to get to work or school but can't afford one, ChangingGears may be able to help — they assist people moving out of poverty to make vehicle ownership possible.

organizations.name: LISC Cincinnati
organizations.mission: If your community organization needs capital or technical assistance for housing development or neighborhood projects, LISC provides lending and support to CDCs and developers in underserved Cincinnati neighborhoods.

organizations.name: Cincinnati Development Fund
organizations.mission: If you run a small business or community development organization in an underserved area and need financing, CDF provides loans that traditional banks won't make.

organizations.name: Greater Cincinnati Voter Collaborative
organizations.mission: If you're not registered to vote, or want to help register others in your community, GCVC runs year-round registration drives and civic training across Cincinnati neighborhoods.

organizations.name: League of Women Voters — Cincinnati Area
organizations.mission: Before an election, check LWV for nonpartisan voter guides and candidate comparisons — they host forums and publish endorsement-free information on local races and ballot issues.
